What is the speed of sound?
The speed of sound varies depending on the medium. In dry air at 20 degrees Celsius, it is approximately 343 meters per second (1,125 feet per second).
What is the tallest mountain in the solar system?

Tell me about the history of the Eiffel Tower.

The Eiffel Tower was built for the 1889 World's Fair (Exposition Universelle) in Paris. Designed by Gustave Eiffel's company, it was intended as a temporary structure but was kept because its radiotelephony antenna proved useful for military communications. Originally criticized by some artists, it has since become a global icon of Paris and France.

How many bones are in the human body?
An adult human body has 206 bones.

What is the boiling point of mercury?
The boiling point of mercury at standard atmospheric pressure (1 atmosphere) is approximately 356.7 degrees Celsius (674 degrees Fahrenheit).

What is the chemical formula for table salt?
The chemical formula for table salt (common salt) is NaCl, which stands for sodium chloride.
